message:
fix(sync): correct notify baton-passing when broadcast follows notify_one

The Notified::poll path was dropping the in-flight notify_one baton when
a later broadcast covered the same waiter set. Waiters arriving after the
broadcast would never receive the original baton, causing a lost wake-up.

Now, when a waiter detects a generation change (broadcast) but was already
individually notified, it forwards the baton to any post-broadcast waiters
via pass_baton_if_waiter_exists() before returning Ready.

Additional changes:
- Update raptorq wavefront pipeline benchmark data (fresh run with
  improved p50/p95/p99 latencies across all block configs)
- Whitelist raptorq_track_e_gf256_multiscenario_refresh_v3.json in .gitignore
- Fix explorer test: update expected seed from 99 to 10 to match
  actual filter behavior
- Add "pending" to VALID_UNIT_OUTCOMES in test_log_schema
- Reformat buffer.rs waker extraction (cosmetic)
- Suppress clippy::too_many_lines on retransmission test

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
